meta.description: Spec-driven development task list for a local LLM. Each step is self-contained: it includes the full context, exact files to modify, acceptance criteria, and test requirements so the LLM can execute the step without reading the rest of the codebase.
meta.execution_model: Steps within the same phase may be executed concurrently. Phases must be executed in order. Each step must pass its acceptance_criteria before the next phase begins.

phase_1_critical_bugs.description: Fix two runtime bugs that can silently corrupt data or produce wrong behavior. Must be completed before any other phase.

steps.title: Remove dead query in lineage_page()

context.problem: lineage.py contains a 3-line block that calls Q.list_files_for_job('braille', -1) using job_id=-1 as a placeholder. The result is assigned to the variable `links` which is never used. This is a leftover comment block from an incomplete refactor. It executes a real database query on every page load, returning no rows but wasting a round-trip. The correct per-job file lookup already exists in the for-loop block that follows immediately after.
context.root_cause: Incomplete refactor — the developer wrote a placeholder comment and loop then wrote the correct implementation below but did not delete the placeholder.
context.impact: One unnecessary DB query on every lineage page load. No data corruption, but misleading code.

locate_block.instruction: Find the block that matches ALL of the following lines appearing consecutively (approximately lines 50-52):
lines_to_find: links = Q.list_files_for_job("braille", -1)  # placeholder; we iterate all

change.description: Delete the entire 3-line block identified above. Do not modify any surrounding code. The for-loop that follows (which iterates braille_jobs and lp_jobs correctly) must remain untouched.

acceptance_criteria: The string 'job_id=-1' or 'list_files_for_job("braille", -1)' does not appear anywhere in lineage.py after the change.
The string 'list_files_for_job' still appears at least twice in lineage.py (used correctly in the braille_jobs and lp_jobs for-loops).
The variable name `links` does not appear anywhere in lineage.py after the change.
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.ui.lineage import lineage_page'` produces no ImportError.
The function `lineage_page` still exists in lineage.py.

test_requirement.description: No new test needed — existing import smoke test is sufficient. Verify with grep.
verify_commands: grep -n 'links = Q' accessibility_mgr/ui/lineage.py || echo 'PASS: not found'

steps.title: Fix delete_file_object() to handle absolute artifact paths

context.problem: When ingest_file() is called with a project_title argument, it stores the file under artifacts/<project>/ and saves the ABSOLUTE path (e.g. /home/user/project/artifacts/Spring2026/FrAn_LegacyJr_Grade7_Science.brf) in the stored_path column of file_object. When delete_file_object() later tries to remove that file, it does: `stored = FILES_DIR / row['stored_path']`. In Python, joining an absolute path using the / operator discards the left operand, so `Path('/some/dir') / '/absolute/path'` returns `Path('/absolute/path')`. This means the delete DOES accidentally work on CPython/POSIX — but it is relying on undefined/platform-specific behavior, produces a confusing code path, and will fail silently on some platforms or if the behavior changes.
context.root_cause: ingest_file() was updated to support artifact storage with absolute paths, but delete_file_object() was not updated to handle both relative and absolute stored_path values.
context.impact: File deletion may silently fail or behave incorrectly on non-POSIX platforms. Code is confusing and non-obvious.

replacement_code.description: Replace the path resolution logic with an explicit absolute-vs-relative check.

acceptance_criteria: The string `FILES_DIR / row["stored_path"]` no longer appears in queries.py.
The string `_sp.is_absolute()` appears in the delete_file_object function body.
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.db.queries import delete_file_object'` produces no ImportError.
A call to delete_file_object() with a row whose stored_path is an absolute path correctly resolves to that absolute path.
A call to delete_file_object() with a row whose stored_path is a relative filename correctly resolves to FILES_DIR / filename.

test_requirement.description: Add a test to tests/test_queries.py named test_delete_file_object_paths that verifies both the absolute-path and relative-path cases. Use tmp_path and monkeypatching as established in the existing fresh_db fixture.
test_outline: Create a temp file at tmp_path / 'test_relative.brf'
Monkeypatch FILES_DIR and ARTIFACTS_DIR to tmp_path subdirs
Call ingest_file() WITHOUT project_title -> stored_path is relative -> verify file is deleted when delete_file_object() is called
Create a second temp file
Call ingest_file() WITH project_title -> stored_path is absolute -> verify file is deleted when delete_file_object() is called
After each delete call, assert the file does not exist on disk
After each delete call, assert get_file_object() returns None

phase_2_missing_file.description: Create the missing models/inventory.py and update models/__init__.py so that all legacy imports resolve cleanly. Neither file is reachable from the running app, but broken imports block test runs and future wiring.

steps.title: Create accessibility_mgr/models/inventory.py

context.problem: Two files import from models/inventory.py which does not exist: ui/categories.py (imports Category) and services/inventory_service.py (imports InventoryItem, InventoryTransaction). This causes an ImportError if either file is ever imported. They are not in PAGE_DEFINITIONS and not reachable from the active app, but they break `import` completeness checks and any future attempt to wire them.
context.root_cause: The project migrated from SQLAlchemy to raw SQLite queries but the legacy UI and service files were not updated or removed. The SQLAlchemy model file was never written.
context.impact: ImportError if either file is imported. Blocks future wiring of categories or inventory service.

file_requirements.module_docstring: Legacy SQLAlchemy inventory models. These are retained for import compatibility with ui/categories.py and services/inventory_service.py. The active application uses the SQLite-native queries layer in db/queries.py instead.
imports_needed: from sqlalchemy import Integer, String, Float, Text, ForeignKey, DateTime
from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship

classes.name: Category
classes.docstring: A named grouping for inventory items.

classes.name: InventoryItem
classes.docstring: A tracked inventory item with a current stock quantity.

classes.name: InventoryTransaction
classes.docstring: An audit record of a quantity change on an InventoryItem.

acceptance_criteria: File accessibility_mgr/models/inventory.py exists.
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.models.inventory import Category, InventoryItem, InventoryTransaction'` produces no ImportError.
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.ui.categories import categories_page'` produces no ImportError.
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.services.inventory_service import InventoryService'` produces no ImportError.
All three classes (Category, InventoryItem, InventoryTransaction) are defined in the file.
Each class inherits from Base (imported from ..db.database).

test_requirement.description: Add a test to tests/test_queries.py named test_legacy_model_imports that verifies the import chain resolves without error.
test_outline: Import Category, InventoryItem, InventoryTransaction from accessibility_mgr.models.inventory
Assert all three are not None
Assert Category.__tablename__ == 'categories'
Assert InventoryItem.__tablename__ == 'inventory_items'
Assert InventoryTransaction.__tablename__ == 'inventory_transactions'

steps.title: Update accessibility_mgr/models/__init__.py to export inventory models

context.problem: models/__init__.py currently only exports the four asset models (Asset, AssetMetadata, Project, WorkflowEvent). After step 2.1 creates models/inventory.py, the three inventory models should be exported from the package __init__ for consistency.
context.impact: Without this, `from accessibility_mgr.models import Category` fails even after 2.1 is complete.

steps.current_content:
"""SQLAlchemy-era model package used by the documentation reference pages."""

from .assets import Asset, AssetMetadata, Project, WorkflowEvent

__all__ = ["Asset", "AssetMetadata", "Project", "WorkflowEvent"]
steps.replacement_content:
"""SQLAlchemy-era model package used by the documentation reference pages."""

from .assets import Asset, AssetMetadata, Project, WorkflowEvent
from .inventory import Category, InventoryItem, InventoryTransaction

__all__ = [
    "Asset",
    "AssetMetadata",
    "Project",
    "WorkflowEvent",
    "Category",
    "InventoryItem",
    "InventoryTransaction",
]
acceptance_criteria: Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.models import Category, InventoryItem, InventoryTransaction'` produces no ImportError.
The __all__ list in models/__init__.py contains all 7 model class names.
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.models import Asset'` still works (existing exports not broken).

test_requirement.description: Covered by step 2.1 test. No additional test needed.
verify_commands: python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.models import Category, InventoryItem, InventoryTransaction; print("PASS")'
python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.models import Asset, AssetMetadata, Project, WorkflowEvent; print("PASS")'

phase_3_seed_data_cleanup.description: Remove the orphaned print workflow_step seed rows from schema.py so the seeded data matches what complete_step() actually supports.

steps.title: Remove orphaned print workflow steps from seed data

context.problem: The INSERT OR IGNORE INTO workflow_step block in schema.py seeds 5 rows for job_type='print' (designed, sliced, printed, inspected, delivered). However, complete_step() in queries.py only supports job_type values 'braille', 'lp_ebraille', and 'tactile'. Calling complete_step('print', ...) raises ValueError. The print_jobs_page does not use step completion — it uses a boolean successful flag. The seeded print steps exist in the database but can never be activated or completed through any application code path. They appear in the Admin -> Workflow Steps panel, creating confusion.
context.decision: Remove the 5 print rows from seed data (Option B). The print job model uses success/fail semantics, not discrete step tracking. If step-level print tracking is added in the future, it should be done as a deliberate feature with schema column additions and _STEP_TABLES support added simultaneously.
context.root_cause: Seed data was written speculatively for a feature that was never implemented in queries.py.

locate_rows_to_remove.instruction: Within the INSERT OR IGNORE INTO workflow_step (...) VALUES block, find and remove the 5 tuples whose first element is 'print':
locate_rows_to_remove.note: The trailing semicolon moves to the last remaining row after deletion. Ensure the INSERT statement remains syntactically valid SQL.

acceptance_criteria: The string "'print'" does not appear in any INSERT OR IGNORE INTO workflow_step tuple in schema.py.
The INSERT OR IGNORE INTO workflow_step block remains syntactically valid SQL (no trailing comma on last row, semicolon present).
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.db.schema import init_db'` produces no SyntaxError or ImportError.
The workflow_step INSERT block still contains all rows for job_type 'braille', 'lp_ebraille', and 'tactile' (14 rows total: 5 + 5 + 4).
Running init_db() on a fresh database results in 14 workflow_step rows (not 19).

test_requirement.description: Add to tests/test_queries.py a test named test_no_print_workflow_steps_seeded.
test_outline: Call init_db() via the fresh_db fixture
Call Q.list_workflow_steps(job_type='print', active_only=False)
Assert the returned list is empty
Call Q.list_workflow_steps(active_only=False)
Assert len(result) == 14

phase_4_dashboard_hardening.description: Replace fragile inline __import__ calls in dashboard Quick Launch buttons with safe module-level references.

steps.title: Replace __import__ Quick Launch buttons with direct module imports

context.problem: dashboard.py Quick Launch buttons use runtime __import__ calls: `on_click=lambda: __import__('accessibility_mgr.ui.braille_jobs', fromlist=['braille_jobs_page']).braille_jobs_page(content_area)`. If any of the 5 target modules fails to import for any reason (syntax error, dependency issue), the button click silently does nothing — no error is shown to the user and no exception is raised to the log. All 5 target modules (braille_jobs, lp_ebraille, tactile_graphics) are already imported at application startup via PAGE_DEFINITIONS, so they are always available in sys.modules.
context.root_cause: Likely copy-pasted from a template or generated without awareness of the startup import context.
context.impact: Silent failures during development if a module has an error. Code is harder to read and static analysis tools cannot resolve the references.

step_1.description: Add three import statements at the top of dashboard.py, after the existing imports block.
step_1.placement: After the line `from .components import PRIORITY_COLORS, section_header`

step_2.description: Replace each __import__ lambda with a direct reference. Find the Quick Launch button block and replace all 5 on_click lambdas.

acceptance_criteria: The string `__import__` does not appear anywhere in dashboard.py.
Three import statements for braille_jobs, lp_ebraille, and tactile_graphics appear near the top of dashboard.py.
All 5 Quick Launch button on_click lambdas reference the module-level aliases (_braille_jobs_mod, _lp_ebraille_mod, _tactile_mod).
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.ui.dashboard import dashboard_page'` produces no ImportError.
Running `ruff check accessibility_mgr/ui/dashboard.py` reports no F401 (unused import) errors for the new imports.

test_requirement.description: No behavioral test needed. Verify with grep and import check.

phase_5_lineage_completeness.description: Extend lineage_page() to include tactile graphics and print jobs in the DAG, so all job types with linked files appear in the provenance graph.

steps.title: Add tactile and print jobs to lineage DAG builder

context.problem: lineage_page() builds a Mermaid DAG connecting jobs to their file_object records. It only queries braille_jobs and lp_jobs. Files attached to tactile_graphics_job or print_job via job_file_link are invisible in the lineage view even though the data exists in the database.
context.root_cause: The two new job type dicts were not added when the page was originally written.
context.impact: Incomplete provenance graph — tactile and print job files do not appear in the lineage viewer.

change.description: After the lines that define `braille_jobs` and `lp_jobs` dicts, add equivalent dicts for tactile and print jobs. Then add two for-loops (mirroring the existing braille and lp loops) that build DAG edges for tactile and print job files.

step_1_add_dicts.instruction: Find the two lines that build braille_jobs and lp_jobs dicts:
existing_lines: braille_jobs = {j["id"]: j["title"] for j in Q.list_braille_jobs()}
lp_jobs = {j["id"]: j["title"] for j in Q.list_lp_jobs()}
add_after: tactile_jobs = {j["id"]: j["title"] for j in Q.list_tactile_jobs()}
print_jobs = {j["id"]: j.get("object_name") or j.get("file_name") or f"Print #{j['id']}" for j in Q.list_print_jobs()}

step_2_add_loops.instruction: After the existing lp_jobs for-loop block (which builds edges between lp jobs and files), add two new for-loop blocks with identical structure:
step_2_add_loops.shape_syntax: Use the same shape as lp jobs: `[/"Label: title"/]`

acceptance_criteria: Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.ui.lineage import lineage_page'` produces no ImportError.
The strings 'tactile_jobs' and 'print_jobs' appear in lineage.py.
The string 'Q.list_tactile_jobs()' appears in lineage.py.
The string 'Q.list_print_jobs()' appears in lineage.py.
The job_file_link type strings 'tactile' and 'print' appear in the new loop blocks.
Node prefix strings 'TG' and 'PJ' appear in lineage.py (for Mermaid node IDs).
The function still uses `seen_edges` set to deduplicate DAG entries.

test_requirement.description: Add to tests/test_queries.py a test named test_lineage_covers_all_job_types that verifies files linked to tactile and print jobs appear in list_files_for_job() calls.
test_outline: Add a tactile job via Q.add_tactile_job()
Create a temp file and ingest it via Q.ingest_file()
Link the file to the tactile job via Q.link_file_to_job(fid, 'tactile', jid)
Call Q.list_files_for_job('tactile', jid) and assert the file appears
Add a printer via Q.add_printer(), add a print job via Q.add_print_job()
Create a second temp file and ingest it
Link it to the print job via Q.link_file_to_job(fid2, 'print', pjid)
Call Q.list_files_for_job('print', pjid) and assert the file appears

phase_6_test_coverage.description: Close the most critical gaps in tests/test_queries.py. All new tests must use the existing fresh_db fixture pattern (tmp_path + monkeypatch of DB_PATH, PRINTS_DIR, FILES_DIR, ARTIFACTS_DIR).

phase_7_roadmap_features.description: Implement the two highest-value not-yet-built features from the README roadmap: a Reports/Analytics page, and stub wiring for Authentication. These are new features, not bug fixes.

steps.title: Create accessibility_mgr/ui/reports.py with a basic analytics page

context.problem: No reports or analytics page exists despite being listed as a planned feature in README.md. Operators have no way to see aggregate statistics, job throughput, inventory consumption trends, or QA pass rates without running manual database queries.
context.scope: This step creates a read-only summary page. No new database tables or query functions are needed — it uses existing list_* functions.

page_sections.name: Production Summary
page_sections.description: Counts of total/in-progress/complete jobs per type (braille, large_print, ebraille, epub3_daisy, tactile, print). Display as stat cards.

page_sections.name: Inventory Health
page_sections.description: Table of all filament with quantity_g and a LOW badge if under 100g. Table of all paper with quantity and a LOW badge if under 50 sheets. Total filament grams consumed (sum of filament_used_g across all print jobs).

page_sections.name: QA Pass Rate
page_sections.description: For each QA tool, show total runs, pass count, fail count, and pass rate percentage. Use the last 50 QA runs.

page_sections.name: Recent Activity
page_sections.description: Last 10 metadata events across all jobs, showing event_datetime, job_type, event_type, event_outcome, and detail.
data_sources: Direct query: SELECT * FROM metadata_event ORDER BY event_datetime DESC LIMIT 10 — add a new query function list_recent_events(limit=10) to queries.py

new_query_function_required.docstring: Return the most recent metadata_event rows across all jobs, newest first.
new_query_function_required.sql: SELECT * FROM metadata_event ORDER BY event_datetime DESC LIMIT ?

wire_into_app.instruction: Add a new entry to PAGE_DEFINITIONS in the Overview group, between Dashboard and Search:

new_entry.name: Reports
new_entry.description: Production analytics and inventory health summary

acceptance_criteria: File accessibility_mgr/ui/reports.py exists and defines reports_page(content_area).
The function list_recent_events() exists in queries.py and is in __all__.
The Reports entry appears in PAGE_DEFINITIONS in app.py.
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.ui.reports import reports_page'` produces no ImportError.
Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.db.queries import list_recent_events'` produces no ImportError.
The page renders at least 4 distinct sections (Production Summary, Inventory Health, QA Pass Rate, Recent Activity).

test_requirement.description: Add to tests/test_queries.py a test named test_list_recent_events.
test_outline: Add a braille job and call complete_step to generate events
Call Q.list_recent_events(limit=5)
Assert the list is not empty
Assert each item has keys: event_type, job_type, job_id, event_datetime
Assert results are ordered newest-first (event_datetime descending)

steps.title: Wire authentication_page into PAGE_DEFINITIONS as an Admin sub-item

context.problem: authentication_page() exists in ui/authentication.py and AuthService exists in services/auth_service.py, but the page is unreachable because it is not in PAGE_DEFINITIONS. The current auth is a non-persistent user-switcher stub — wiring it in makes the stub visible and sets the foundation for future real auth implementation.
context.scope: This step only wires the existing page into the sidebar. It does NOT implement real session auth — that remains a future item. The page will show the current hardcoded user list and allow switching.

Running `python -c 'from accessibility_mgr.ui.authentication import authentication_page'` produces no ImportError.

for_local_llm.model_recommendation: Use a code-capable model with at least 32k context (e.g. Qwen2.5-Coder-32B, DeepSeek-Coder-V2, CodeLlama-34B). Each step is designed to fit within a 8k token prompt when combined with the relevant source file.
for_local_llm.one_step_at_a_time: Send one step per prompt. Do not batch multiple steps. Wait for acceptance_criteria verification before proceeding.
for_local_llm.verification_step: After each step, run the verify_commands or test_requirement before marking status as DONE.

phase_ordering.rule: Complete all steps in phase_1 before starting phase_2. Steps within the same phase that have no depends_on may be run concurrently.

completion_criteria.phase_1_complete: Steps 1.1 and 1.2 both have status DONE and all acceptance_criteria verified.

completion_criteria.phase_6_complete: Steps 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 all DONE and pytest passes with 0 failures.
completion_criteria.phase_7_complete: Steps 7.1 and 7.2 DONE and Reports page visible in sidebar.
completion_criteria.project_complete: All 12 steps DONE, pytest green, ruff check passes on all modified files.
